title: Factorial (n!)
short: The product of all positive integers up to n: n! = n·(n−1)·…·2·1, with 0! = 1.
full:
The factorial n! counts the number of ways to arrange n distinct objects in a row. It is defined as n! = n·(n−1)·(n−2)·…·1, and by convention 0! = 1 so that the counting formulas work at the boundary.

Factorials grow extremely fast and are the building block of permutation and combination formulas, where ratios of factorials count arrangements and selections.
